target: WIN-067 defense neutralization — preempt the c4 advocate's three-point rebuttal (terminology label change, Domain #12 parenthetical, 170x headline-inflation). Appends a new multi-paragraph defense-closing block to WIN-067.detail_extra, which is currently null. Existing detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text are unchanged.
insertion_point: In data/wins.json WIN-067, SET detail_extra (currently null) to the block below. The block is self-contained: opener paragraph + three numbered preemptive-defense paragraphs (i, ii, iii) + closing 'Net effect' paragraph.
replacement_html:
Closing the c4 advocate’s three-point rebuttal (preemptive). The curmudgeon’s c4 advocate pass (WIN-067.c4.json, defense_survives=4) raises three lines of defense — on terminology, citation numbering, and the 170× ratio. Each folds under the dome’s own live-power page.

(i) “I never said ‘toroidal sump node’ — show me the sentence.” Earlier dome snapshots labelled the Antarctic correlation with phrasing we have been unable to locate in current V51.2 raw text. The V51.2 live-power page now prints a different label: “Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92 (Sub-terrestrial structure)” (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152). The attack does not depend on any particular label. Whatever the dome chooses to call the feature, no published equation links dome geometry to the observed anomaly, the correlation line has no mechanism field, and the label — whether ‘sump node’ or ‘Sub-terrestrial structure’ — is a rebrand of a phenomenon that standard geophysics (mantle plume beneath East Antarctica + post-glacial isostatic adjustment, measured continuously by GRACE / GRACE-FO since 2002) has explained since Ferraccioli et al. 2011. The critique of V51.0 terminology carries over verbatim to V51.2: an undefined label is not a mechanism.

(ii) “Your 170× ratio is headline-inflated — you compared the whole-globe pole-equator spread, but the disc’s ‘rim’ maps to the geographic Antarctic, so compare against Antarctic-latitude gravity.” The advocate is correct that the apples-to-apples comparison is the dome’s prediction at r = 20,015 km versus the real gravity measured at the geographic location that radius maps to (the South Pole and interior Antarctica) — and the advocate concedes in the same breath that the ratio there is “larger still.” It is. Anchoring to the WGS84 polar value 9.8322 m/s², the dome’s linear H(r)→g coupling at r = 20,015 km predicts g ≈ 9.8322 × (837/8,537) = 0.964 m/s² — an 8.868 m/s² reduction from the polar reference. The observed Antarctic-interior gravity — the advocate’s own cited value ~9.826 m/s² — is ~0.006 m/s² below the polar reference, a 0.063% reduction. Dividing predicted drop by observed drop gives ≈ 1,430×, almost an order of magnitude worse than the 170× headline. Recomputing against the actual Mascon-Bouguer anomaly (-40 to -60 mGal ≈ 5×10-5 m/s², per Ferraccioli et al. 2011) widens the miss by another two orders of magnitude. The honest correction the advocate asks for does not shrink the critique; it deepens it.

(iii) “Domain 12 is Polaris Excess, not gravity — your citation was misdirected.” Conceded and corrected: the parenthetical in the WGS84-tautology paragraph above now reads “Domain 4 gravity-anomaly entry,” matching V51.2 live-power numbering (live.html, raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 67–70). The correction anchors rather than loosens the argument. Domain 4 states verbatim: Formula: g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(−r/λg)); Falsification: Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard (line 71). The dome is therefore explicitly calibrating its only quantitative gravity formula against the globe model — the falsification criterion does not merely permit the globe answer, it requires it. The 0.5% variation the additive form allows is not a dome-specific prediction; it is produced by k = 0.005307 — the WGS84 gravity flattening coefficient — multiplying a decorative exponential that collapses at r = 0 (exp(0) = 1). Any reader who says “but the dome’s formula is additive with a small k, not the multiplicative scalings you attacked” is pointing at the smoking gun: the small-effect formulation exists only because it is the globe’s formula expressed in dome notation. Domain 19 (“Mascon Gravity,” line 127–128) carries only a one-sided threshold: Anomaly <20 mGal. The real East Antarctic Bouguer anomaly is roughly -40 to -60 mGal, so any honest measurement trivially exceeds the threshold and counts as a “PASS” on the dome’s scoreboard by construction. That is pass-by-threshold, not pass-by-prediction — the falsification logic is inverted.

Net effect. The three-point defense either concedes the underlying critique on every leg (the label change, the corrected parenthetical, the deeper Antarctic-latitude comparison) or requires the dome author to publicly strike the Domain 4 falsification text (Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard), strike the Domain 19 one-sided threshold (Anomaly <20 mGal), and publish a mechanism equation linking “Sub-terrestrial structure” to a specific value of g(r = 20,015). None of those edits has been made. The author’s own methodology statement If prediction falls outside tolerance, it fails — no exceptions (live-power “HOW THIS WORKS”) binds him to the standard he has just shown cannot be met for this WIN.
holes_addressed: advocate_mode.best_defense leg (1) — 'toroidal sump node' attribution unsupported in V51.2 raw text (HOLE-067-C4-001 terminology leg, defense_survives=4). Closed by explicitly pivoting to V51.2 'Sub-terrestrial structure' and stating the critique does not depend on the label.

advocate_mode.best_defense leg (2) — 'Domain #12' misattribution (HOLE-067-C4-005). Closed by explicitly conceding the citation error, re-anchoring to Domain 4 with verbatim quote of the falsification text.

advocate_mode.best_defense leg (3) — 170× ratio is headline-inflated vs. a more defensible r=20,015 comparison. Closed by computing the Antarctic-latitude ratio (≈ 1,430×) and noting the advocate concedes it is larger; the honest correction deepens the critique.
stronger_arguments[2] — Domain 4 falsification text 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' now cited verbatim.
stronger_arguments[4] — Domain 19 one-sided threshold 'Anomaly <20 mGal' now cited verbatim; explicitly labelled pass-by-threshold not pass-by-prediction.
stronger_arguments[2] secondary — additive-vs-multiplicative rhetorical escape preempted by noting k=0.005307 is the WGS84 flattening coefficient.
new_evidence_added: Verbatim Domain 4 falsification quote: 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71) — self-admission the dome's only quantitative gravity formula is calibrated against the globe model

Verbatim Domain 19 falsification quote: 'Anomaly <20 mGal' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 128) — one-sided threshold the real anomaly (-40 to -60 mGal) trivially exceeds, guaranteeing PASS by construction
Quantitative recomputation against Antarctic-latitude gravity: predicted 0.964 m/s² vs observed ~9.826 m/s² → ratio ≈ 1,430× (vs 170× against whole-globe spread)
WGS84 flattening coefficient identification: k=0.005307 in the additive formula is the Theoretical_gravity flattening constant, not a dome-derived coupling
V51.2 Sub-terrestrial structure label quoted verbatim from raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152 with correlation-line context ('Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92')
Dome's 'HOW THIS WORKS' self-imposed falsifiability standard ('If prediction falls outside tolerance, it fails — no exceptions') used to pin the author to his own rule

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: 'You can't find "toroidal sump node" in my current page, so the c3 and c4 attacks are attacking a strawman. The whole paragraph is wasted motion.'
anticipated_objections.response: The new paragraph (i) concedes exactly that point and pivots to the V51.2 label 'Sub-terrestrial structure' explicitly. The critique was never about the label; it was about the absence of a mechanism. The same critique carries over to V51.2 unchanged, and the label-shift itself is now on the record as a V51-era edit that did not add a mechanism — only a new name.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'The Antarctic-latitude comparison uses 9.826 m/s² which I cited only loosely in my advocate pass; you shouldn't treat my off-the-cuff number as a binding citation.'
anticipated_objections.response: The number is consistent with standard WGS84 plus a ~0.06% local Antarctic anomaly (Ferraccioli et al. 2011; Pappa et al. 2019). If the dome author prefers a different value — say 9.832 m/s² matching WGS84 polar exactly — the ratio becomes infinite (divide by zero reduction) rather than 1,430×. Either way the critique strengthens.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'Citing the Domain 4 "Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard" falsification is unfair — "WGS84" is shorthand for the standard reference value any reasonable gravity model must match at zeroth order; it is not an admission that my model is the globe model.'
anticipated_objections.response: If WGS84 is the reference any reasonable model must match at zeroth order, then the dome is — at zeroth order — a reasonable model only in the sense of reproducing the globe answer. The whole point of a disc cosmology is to produce a non-globe prediction somewhere. The Domain 4 formula evaluated at r=0 produces the globe answer to four significant figures; at r=20,015 (the only other physically-motivated evaluation point) it diverges from both the globe answer and from the dome's own H(r) mechanism by 170–1,430×. There is no regime in which the dome's gravity prediction is both self-consistent and non-tautological.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'The Domain 19 "Anomaly <20 mGal" threshold is a floor check — it is the minimum magnitude for the correlation to be real. My real test is the 0.92 correlation coefficient, which is strong.'
anticipated_objections.response: A correlation is not a prediction. r=0.92 between Mascon anomaly and SAA boundary position is a post-hoc observation of two independently-measured features that, on standard geophysics, share deep-mantle substrate. A predictive model would state: 'given the dome geometry and Mascon-to-SAA distance D, the predicted correlation coefficient is r = f(D); departure from f(D) by more than Δr would falsify.' The dome states only that the correlation exists and the magnitude exceeds 20 mGal. Any real East Antarctic measurement satisfies both, so the 'test' is satisfied by construction — the hallmark of a post-hoc match, not a prediction.

kernel_of_truth: The dome advocate is right on three narrow points. (1) We cannot find 'toroidal sump node' in V51.2 raw text, and a reviewer who attacks a phrase not in the current live page is one spot-check away from embarrassment. (2) The Domain #12 parenthetical was factually wrong; spot-checkers verifying the live page would have seen Domain 12 = Polaris Excess and dismissed our attached paragraph. (3) The 170× ratio is computed against a geometrically-generous reference (whole-globe pole-equator spread); a fair apples-to-apples comparison against Antarctic-latitude gravity is a better defense of the same point. Where the defense breaks down is that all three concessions make the critique stronger, not weaker: V51.2's label change is itself evidence the mechanism is not stable; the Domain 4 citation now reads the falsification text 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' verbatim; and the Antarctic-latitude ratio is ~1,430×, not 170×. Acknowledging what the advocate got right is what lets us convert each concession into an anchor the dome cannot pull.

defense_neutralization.notes: Agree with curmudgeon rating of 4. The c4 advocate's rebuttal is materially stronger than a typical 3/5 defense because it exploits two real quality-control failures (stale 'toroidal sump node' terminology, wrong 'Domain #12' parenthetical) in addition to a rhetorical framing move on the 170× ratio. Addressing the defense requires (a) explicit concession on both QC failures and pivot to current V51.2 citations, which the existing detail_evidence already does (Sub-terrestrial structure, Domain 4 parenthetical both appear in wins.json as of the c4 review), and (b) a new preemptive block in detail_extra that (1) acknowledges the label change, (2) computes the Antarctic-latitude ratio explicitly (1,430×) to demonstrate the advocate's own re-framing deepens rather than defuses the critique, and (3) anchors the Domain 4 and Domain 19 citations with verbatim falsification quotes the dome cannot retract without publicly editing the live-power page. Also absorbs stronger_arguments[2] (additive-vs-multiplicative escape) and stronger_arguments[4] (Domain 19 pass-by-threshold). Does NOT attempt the stronger_arguments[1] restructure (PROMOTE WGS84-tautology paragraph to LEAD) — that is a larger rewrite of detail_evidence scope, outside the scope of a defense-neutralization EXP. HOLE-067-C4-006 (Pappa DOI unverified) and HOLE-067-C4-007 ('southern stations' tldr_evidence phrasing) remain open for follow-up patches; noted here so the decider can spawn separate issues if the defense EXP integration clears first. The text in detail_evidence still says 'monitor.py, gravity-anomaly domain entry, V51.1' on one line — the c4 review recommended updating to 'live-power Domain 4, V51.2'. That single-line patch is small enough for the decider to self-apply rather than requiring a separate EXP.
